Home » Governor — Republican Primary

Governor — Republican Primary

Seven candidates qualified (James Libby dropped out April 9, endorsed Bush). Ranked-choice voting applies in the primary — rank as many candidates as you want. Most Republican candidates oppose RCV on principle, but some are forming second-choice alliances anyway.

What a Governor does: Proposes and executes Maine's $10 billion state budget — that's your property taxes, your kid's school funding, your road paving, your healthcare. Appoints hundreds of officials. Signs or vetoes every bill. Commands the National Guard. Four-year term. Incumbent Janet Mills (D) is term-limited.

Polling

UNH Pine Tree (May 27)Charles 37% · Bush ~20% · Mason 9% · Midgley 11%
Pan Atlantic (May 20)Charles 36% · Bush 20% · Mason 13% · Midgley 2%
2nd choice (UNH)Midgley 18% · Bush 13% · Jones 10% · Mason 9%
3rd choice (UNH)Mason 18% · Wessels 14% · Bush 8% · Midgley 8%
GOP convention strawCharles won (April 25)

UNH Survey Center · Portland Press Herald

RCV Dynamics

This is an RCV race — simple plurality does NOT win. If no candidate tops 50%, lowest performers are eliminated and ballots redistribute. Second and third choices matter. Two alliances: Midgley↔Jones (mutual second choice) and Wessels→Bush. Charles and Mason urge supporters to rank themselves only — limiting their RCV path.

Campaign Finance

Bush loaned $1.1M. Midgley loaned $750K, $446K cash on hand (highest in field). Mason: $4.2M+ in outside PAC spending (Uihlein/Klingenstein). Charles: $220K loaned, relies on small donors and social media. McCarthy, Jones, Wessels: minimal self-funding. Maine Public, May 15

Bobby Charles

Republican
Leeds/Wayne · Attorney, former US Asst. Secretary of State (2003-05) · Won GOP convention straw poll · Ballotpedia

Platform

State Budget
Proposed cutting $4 billion from the state budget. Mason: "that would tank the Maine economy." Target non-competitive contracts, "illegal alien" housing, EBT cards, MaineCare waste.
Maine Morning Star, May 13
Taxes
Eliminate income tax over 4 years. Expand homestead exemption.
Maine Morning Star, May 5
Immigration
"This is Maine, not Somalia. I will deport the illegals, ban Sharia law and work to block more resettlements." Released 3-part "Islamification" plan: ban courts from enforcing foreign/religious code, direct AG to restrict terrorist orgs from buying property, modeled on Florida/Texas laws.
Energy
Opposes forcing ratepayers to subsidize solar: "I don't have to pay for your solar."
RCV Strategy
Urges supporters to rank Charles only — no second choice. Accused candidates making RCV alliances of "attempting to manipulate" the primary.

Background

Grew up in Wayne. Studied economics at Oxford, Columbia Law. Clerked on US Court of Appeals. Worked for Reagan Administration. Joined Navy as intelligence officer just before 9/11. Asst. Secretary of State for International Narcotics 2003-05 (~18 months). Has spent 25 of the last 27 years in the Washington DC area. Won the GOP convention straw poll April 25.

Controversies

A dozen federal audits criticized his State Dept bureau for accepting contractor claims without verification, paying invoices before work was contracted, allowing unauthorized construction (including a swimming pool at Adnan Palace in Baghdad). Opium production in Afghanistan grew during his tenure. Skipped first two televised debates; rivals mocked him — Bush: "come out of the Joe Biden bunker." His "Islamification" comments drew sharp criticism from Dem candidate Hannah Pingree and fellow Republicans.

Endorsements

Won GOP convention straw poll. No major named endorsements reported. Trump has not endorsed any candidate.

Jonathan Bush

Republican
Cape Elizabeth · Founder of Athenahealth ($17B, 1,000+ jobs in Belfast) & Zus Health · Former Army medic, Desert Storm · Nephew of Pres. George H.W. Bush · Ballotpedia

Platform

Message
"Shrink taxes, shred crazy laws, and shrink Augusta." Would declare an "economic growth emergency" as first act.
The Maine Wire
Taxes
$1 billion income tax cut. Lower taxes by attracting businesses, making state less dependent on taxing retirees/workers/homeowners.
Healthcare
"Health care is my jam." Vows to cut and audit MaineCare. Says fraud is "rampant." Would decrease regulations on childcare providers.
Maine Public, May 15
Energy
"Wind and solar subsidies go into the shredder. This entire environmental virtue signaling on working people's expense must go."
Maine Morning Star, May 13
Housing
"I love the housing issue — it's like a greatest hits album of crazy Democratic ideology." Supports cutting regulations.

Background

Nephew of George H.W. Bush, cousin of George W. Bush. Wesleyan '93, Harvard Business School '97. Served as Army combat medic during Desert Storm; worked as EMT in New Orleans. Co-founded Athenahealth (1997), a healthcare tech platform. Resigned as CEO 2018 during activist investor campaign. Founded Zus Health (Boston) in 2020. Athenahealth employed 900+ Mainers at peak. 7 children.

Endorsements

State Sen. Jim Libby (who dropped out of race after failing to qualify for Clean Election funding). Robert Wessels (fellow candidate) endorsed Bush as second choice — Bush offered him "CD2 Grassroots Chair." Former state senator (western mountains) support letter in Press Herald.

Garrett Mason

Republican
Lisbon · Former Maine Senate Majority Leader under LePage · Ran for governor in 2018 · Only candidate who has held elected office in Augusta · Ballotpedia

Platform

Taxes
Eliminate income tax. Helped cut income tax by ~1% when Republicans had full control. Wants $1B income tax cut ($2,000/family, 12% of biennial budget). Called Charles' $4B proposal "click bait."
Maine Morning Star, May 5
MaineCare
Voted against Medicaid expansion as senator. Now wants to repeal it — would remove ~100,000 low-income Mainers from program.
Maine Public, May 15
Healthcare
Suggested using National Guard to help fill gaps in rural health care.
Education
School choice. Reduce "wokeness" in schools. "We have all this focus on people who are caring for kids, and absolutely none on people who are not taking care of kids that are wards of the state."
Energy
"All of the above energy approach — the cheapest possible thing."
Maine Morning Star, May 13
RCV Strategy
Urges supporters to rank Mason only — no second choice. Opposes RCV on principle.

Background

Elected to Maine Senate at 25 in 2010 Republican wave. Became Majority Leader during LePage administration. Ran for governor in 2018, finished second in GOP primary. Left office 2018; started a public affairs/lobbying company. Only candidate with Augusta elected experience.

Endorsements

40+ sitting and former state legislators — most legislative endorsements of any candidate. House Minority Leader Billy Bob Faulkingham. Rep. Jeff Ducharme (ranking Republican on Appropriations). Massive outside spending: Restoration of America PAC (funded by billionaire Richard Uihlein and Thomas Klingenstein) spent $4.2M+ supporting Mason.

Controversies

"Establishment" label is toxic in 2026 GOP primary. Lobbying after leaving office draws "insider" attacks from rivals. Opposes RCV but still competing in an RCV race — limiting second-choice ballots hurts his path to victory.

Ben Midgley

Republican
Kennebunkport · Former president of Planet Fitness · Founding partner, Crunch Franchising · Most cash on hand in field · Ballotpedia

Platform

Welfare
"First thing is welfare reform so people aren't stuck on the cliff and they have a path to prosperity." Supports MaineCare work requirements. Won't commit to bipartisan budgets.
Maine Morning Star, May 5
Approach
"The next governor should be a seasoned and experienced chief executive officer, which I am." CEO-style governance. Job creation is his differentiator: delivering "results" not "proving I'm the reddest."
Education
Against "wokeness" in schools. Supports school competition. Advocates decreasing regulations on childcare providers.
Energy
"I'm fine with renewables, as long as they don't do it on the backs of Maine rate payers." Expedite DEP/DOT permitting.
Maine Morning Star, May 13

Background

Former president of Planet Fitness; founding partner of Crunch Franchising. Grew company from scratch to 15,000 jobs nationwide. Grew up in Maine. No college degree. Former Democrat — switched about a decade ago, which rivals use against him. Lauren LePage (former governor's daughter) is a Midgley strategist.

Endorsements

Ann LePage (former first lady, wife of Paul LePage). House Minority Leader Billy Bob Faulkingham: "Some of the greatest Republicans stories in history are people that started off as Democrats." Much of LePage's inner circle coalesced around Midgley. David Jones (fellow candidate) — mutual second-choice endorsement.

RCV Position

Alliance with David Jones (mutual second-choice cross-endorsement). Top second-choice candidate at 18% — strong RCV path to victory despite only 2-11% first-choice polling.

Owen McCarthy

Republican
Gorham · Co-founder of MedRhythms ($60M+ raised, FDA-cleared) · UMaine trustee · Ballotpedia

Platform

Maine 2040 Plan
72-page policy blueprint — most detailed in the race. Three pillars: Opportunity, Affordability, Discipline. Focus on creating 50,000 foundational jobs across Maine.
Maine Morning Star, May 5
Taxes
Cut income taxes 10% for those under $200,000. Long-term goal: eliminate income tax. Veterans with permanent/total disability pay no property taxes.
Maine Morning Star, May 5
Housing
Zoning reform for modular housing. Cut red tape. Invest in trade programs in schools. Supports statewide ban on rent control.
Maine Morning Star, May 13
Approach
Sees himself as most moderate Republican; "people are craving" generational change. Emphasizes systems, execution, incentives, measurable outcomes. Called himself the "working class outsider" candidate — "only without Graham Platner's racism, sexism, communism, and Nazi tattoos. I, in fact, have no tattoos."

Background

Engineer by trade. Co-founded MedRhythms, a Portland health tech company using neuroscience and AI to help stroke/Parkinson's patients regain mobility. Raised $60M+, earned FDA clearance, completed clinical trials. Age 38 — youngest candidate. Husband, father of two young sons. UMaine system trustee.

Electability

Most detailed policy (72 pages), most moderate, youngest — but can't break through in a Trump-aligned primary. 2-4% first-choice polling despite substantial policy substance. Only 1% as second choice.

David Jones

Republican
Falmouth · Owner, F.O. Bailey Real Estate · Independent candidate for governor in 2006 · Ballotpedia

Platform

Property Taxes
Eliminate property taxes entirely. Launch "David's Office of Government Efficiency" (DOGE) — modeled on Elon Musk's federal DOGE — to offset revenue loss. Increase homestead exemption from $25,000 to $300,000 (first year), eventually $500,000.
The Maine Wire
Housing
As a developer, says he's most qualified to increase housing stock. Regulations add $80K-$120K per single-family home. "That's insane."
Budget
Cut $1.5B in first year; $2B total. Acknowledges reality: "if we don't have the House and we don't have the Senate, we can all sit here and make all the promises in the world."
RCV Alliance
Mutual second-choice cross-endorsement with Ben Midgley.

Background

Owns F.O. Bailey Real Estate. Ran for governor as independent in 2006. 10% as second choice in UNH polling — fourth strongest RCV position. Pragmatic about legislative math — acknowledges likely Democratic legislature.

Robert Wessels

Republican
Paris · Former Paris Board of Selectmen (4 years) · Retail manager 14 years · MBA

Platform

Property Taxes
Eliminate property taxes for Mainers' primary residences. Far more specific than most candidates on this issue.
Housing
Streamline building regulations. Cut red tape. Supports statewide ban on rent control. "Our government is currently really hurting the supply side. One of our biggest solutions is to get our government out of the way."
Education
"Stop teaching CRT and DEI in our schools; create competition with school choice."

Background

Retail manager for 14 years. Former Paris Town Selectman (4 years). MBA. Has taught personal finance/budgeting courses in Oxford Hills community for 18 years. Describes himself as "not an establishment candidate." Missed early debate polling thresholds but reached 6% in UNH May 27 poll (above 5% threshold).

RCV Position

Endorsed Jonathan Bush as second choice. Bush offered him "CD2 Grassroots Chair." 14% as third choice in UNH polling.

Comparison

Charles Bush Mason Midgley McCarthy Jones Wessels
Polling (1st) 36-37% ~20% 9-13% 2-11% 2-4% 3-7% 1-6%
Income tax Eliminate in 4 yrs $1B cut Eliminate General cut 10% cut <$200K General cut General cut
Property tax Expand homestead Cut via growth General cut General cut Veterans exempt Eliminate entirely Eliminate for 1st home
Budget cut $4B $1B+ $1B Welfare focus Discipline focus $1.5-2B $2B (or $2M)
MaineCare Work reqs; cut noncitizens Cut & audit; fraud focus Repeal expansion (100K off) Cut; welfare reform Not detailed Phase out Cut fraud
Immigration Deport; ban Sharia Enforcement Enforcement General opposition Not detailed Not detailed Not detailed
Housing Cut regs Cut regs; anti-Dem ideology Cut regs No proposals published Zoning; modular; ban rent ctrl Cut regs ($80-120K/home) Cut red tape; ban rent ctrl
RCV alliance None (rank 1 only) Wessels → Bush None (rank 1 only) ↔ Jones None ↔ Midgley → Bush
Self-funding $220K loan $1.1M loan PAC $4.2M+ $750K loan Minimal Minimal Minimal

Who You'd Face in November

Note: You vote in only one primary. The Republican field is shown below so you can evaluate how this primary's candidates match up in the general election.

Who You'd Face in November

Republican
Bobby Charles: Trump-aligned attorney, won GOP straw poll, leads at 37%. Wants to eliminate income tax in 4 years; cut $4 billion from the budget. Would deport immigrants and ban Sharia law. Skipped multiple debates. Full GOP primary
Jonathan Bush: Healthcare tech entrepreneur (Athenahealth), nephew of President George W. Bush. $1B income tax cut. "Healthcare is my jam" — would declare an "economic growth emergency" day one. Second place at ~20%.
Garrett Mason: Former Senate Majority Leader under LePage, only candidate with Augusta legislative experience. Would eliminate income tax and repeal Medicaid expansion (removing ~100,000 Mainers). Third place at 9-13%. Backed by $4.2M+ in outside PAC spending (Uihlein/Klingenstein).
Ben Midgley: Former Planet Fitness president. LePage inner circle. Top second-choice candidate (18%) via RCV alliance with David Jones. Only $29,000 raised from others despite $750K self-loan.
Owen McCarthy: Health tech founder (MedRhythms), youngest candidate at 38. Most detailed policy doc (72 pages). "Working class outsider" who built business in Maine. Only 2-4% first choice.
David Jones: Real estate developer, Falmouth. Wants to eliminate property taxes entirely. "David's Office of Government Efficiency" (DOGE). Alliance with Midgley. 3-7% first choice.
Robert Wessels: Retail manager, Paris selectman. Most outsider candidate in the field. Endorsed Bush as second choice. Lowest profile, 1-6% first choice.

Sources

Maine Morning Star — GOP debate, May 5 · Maine Morning Star — GOP debate, May 13 · Maine Public — RCV alliances, May 15 · The Maine Wire — Debate coverage

Last updated: May 31, 2026.

South Portland Voter Guide — June 2026. Nonpartisan.

Sources: Maine Secretary of State, South Portland City Clerk.

This guide is an independent project. It does not endorse any candidate.
